description:
Testing-review M1. A mutation tool whose own suite is never mutation-tested cannot demonstrate the property it sells. Self-mutation of MutagenEx.* is refused at runtime (r3, mutation_runner.ex:241), but the lane-fixture/System.cmd harness already boots a child project — run mutagen_ex against a PREFIX-RENAMED copy of lib/mutagen_ex/mutators/ (renamed out of the MutagenEx. prefix, or via the archive-install path with no :mutagen_ex dep).

Fix: add a :dogfood-tagged slow lane asserting a kill-rate floor (e.g. >=90%). Surviving mutants become actionable test gaps (and likely re-confirm H2/M2).

Done: documented dogfooding run; kill-rate floor enforced in a tagged lane; surviving mutants triaged.
title: M1: self-dogfooding lane — run mutagen_ex over its own mutators with a kill-rate floor
